topic: Incident management: PagerDuty vs Opsgenie vs Rootly

messages.content: Need incident management tooling. PagerDuty seems expensive. Alternatives?

Incident management tools comparison:

**PagerDuty**:
- Industry standard, most mature
- On-call scheduling, escalation, automated response
- AI-powered noise reduction
- $21/user/month (Professional)
- Worth it for: large teams, complex escalation needs

**Opsgenie** (Atlassian):
- Similar features to PagerDuty at lower cost
- Integrates with Jira, Confluence
- $9/user/month (Essentials), $19 (Standard)
- Good for: Atlassian shops

**Grafana OnCall** (open-source):
- Free self-hosted, or Grafana Cloud
- On-call schedules, escalation, Slack integration
- Integrates with Grafana alerting natively
- Good for: teams already on Grafana stack

**Rootly / incident.io**:
- Incident management + on-call + status pages
- Slack-native workflows
- Automated incident channels, roles, postmortems
- ~$16-25/user/month
- Good for: Slack-heavy teams

For cost-conscious teams: **Grafana OnCall** (free) + **Better Stack** (status page, free tier). For Atlassian teams: **Opsgenie**. For best-in-class: **PagerDuty** or **incident.io**.
